name: Mantis

attacks.name: scythe cut
attacks.tell: The Mantis swings one of its front claws in a wide arc
attacks.howToAvoid: Dodge back out of arm's reach or block with reinforced shield
attacks.notes: Quick attack that covers a semicircle in front of her; can cut nearby vegetation

attacks.name: devastating jump
attacks.tell: He lowers his body and his elytra vibrate before launching into a long jump.
attacks.howToAvoid: Roll to one side when you see the jump begin, as you will land exactly where you were
attacks.notes: Upon landing, it generates great area damage and can leave the player confused for a few moments.

attacks.name: Mantis Fury
attacks.tell: When his health drops below 25%, he emits a high-pitched screech and his eyes glow red.
attacks.howToAvoid: N/A (activation cannot be avoided); exercise extreme caution from that moment on
attacks.notes: Enters a state of fury: increases its speed and frequency of attacks. Your patterns become less telegraphic and faster

attacks.name: Double X Strike
attacks.tell: Enraged, she crosses both claws and performs two crossed slashes in quick succession in the shape of an
attacks.howToAvoid: Block both slashes quickly or dodge backwards (one side can still hit you due to wide range)
attacks.notes: Characteristic attack in the rage phase; very damaging, but leaves a short gap after completing it where the Mantis is left open

bossPhases.label: Phase 1
bossPhases.summary: The Mantis starts as a pure duel: long scythe slashes and punishing leaps if you stay too linear.
bossPhases.trigger: It begins when she drops from the upper leaves of the pot.

attacks.name: scythe cut
attacks.tell: The Mantis swings one of its front claws in a wide arc
attacks.howToAvoid: Dodge back out of arm's reach or block with reinforced shield
attacks.notes: Quick attack that covers a semicircle in front of her; can cut nearby vegetation

attacks.name: devastating jump
attacks.tell: He lowers his body and his elytra vibrate before launching into a long jump.
attacks.howToAvoid: Roll to one side when you see the jump begin, as you will land exactly where you were
attacks.notes: Upon landing, it generates great area damage and can leave the player confused for a few moments.

attacks.name: Charged Strike
attacks.tell: She delays one scythe briefly before releasing a much heavier slash.
attacks.howToAvoid: Perfect block it on real timing or roll to her side; taking it head-on usually loses the trade.
attacks.notes: It is the move that best punishes rushed counterattacks.

bossPhases.label: Phase 2
bossPhases.summary: With the first scream the fight speeds up. The Mantis gains a multi-hit ground-shredding pattern and cuts the player’s exhaustion recovery.
bossPhases.trigger: She enters at about 80% health with a scream that hits in an area.
newPatterns: The stamina-recovery penalty makes overblocking much more expensive.

attacks.name: Deafening Scream
attacks.tell: She rises up and shakes her body before unleashing a shriek across the whole pot.
attacks.howToAvoid: Back off if you are already inside her hitbox; otherwise prepare the block and do not spend stamina right before it.
attacks.notes: Besides the damage, it makes the rest of the phase much less comfortable for the player.

attacks.name: Ground Shred
attacks.tell: She lowers her scythes and chains several low shredding hits forward.
attacks.howToAvoid: Do not try to block the whole thing on autopilot; leave the line or only take the timings you actually read.
attacks.notes: It inflicts bleed and heavily punishes anyone who stays planted in front of her.

attacks.name: Mantis Fury
attacks.tell: When his health drops below 25%, he emits a high-pitched screech and his eyes glow red.
attacks.howToAvoid: N/A (activation cannot be avoided); exercise extreme caution from that moment on
attacks.notes: Enters a state of fury: increases its speed and frequency of attacks. Your patterns become less telegraphic and faster

bossPhases.label: Phase 3
bossPhases.summary: The final phase hardens every punishment. The Mantis chains double X cuts, vertical shockwaves, and punishes any obvious heal.
bossPhases.trigger: She enters below 40% health with a second scream and far less patience.
newPatterns: The last phase forces short punishes; trying to squeeze every opening usually backfires.

attacks.name: Double X Strike
attacks.tell: Enraged, she crosses both claws and performs two crossed slashes in quick succession in the shape of an
attacks.howToAvoid: Block both slashes quickly or dodge backwards (one side can still hit you due to wide range)
attacks.notes: Characteristic attack in the rage phase; very damaging, but leaves a short gap after completing it where the Mantis is left open

attacks.name: Vertical Shockwave
attacks.tell: She drives her scythes down and kicks up a shockwave that travels along the ground.
attacks.howToAvoid: Jump it or leave the lane before the wave reaches you.
attacks.notes: It exists to shut down heals, reset spacing, and seize the pace again.

attacks.name: Mantis Fury
attacks.tell: When his health drops below 25%, he emits a high-pitched screech and his eyes glow red.
attacks.howToAvoid: N/A (activation cannot be avoided); exercise extreme caution from that moment on
attacks.notes: Enters a state of fury: increases its speed and frequency of attacks. Your patterns become less telegraphic and faster

description: A sleek optional assassin living in the orchid pot on the shed porch. It is one of the most technical bosses in the game, mixing speed, debuffs, and spacing control.
behavior: It alternates close slashes, charged strikes that apply bleed, unblockable leaps, and vertical shockwaves that weaken the player's damage. Each phase adds more resistance, more damage, and heavier punishment to stamina and perfect-block timing.
interactionWithPlayer: It is immediately hostile and seals the arena exit when the fight begins. It forces the player to react differently depending on whether they stay close or far away.
interactionWithCreatures: In NG+ it can summon infused Mini-Mantises as extra support in later phases. They all disappear when the Mantis dies.
strategy: Salty is the key weakness and the Morning Star remains a very strong answer. The fight demands learning which moves are blockable, which are not, and which are best answered by pure lateral movement.
respawnInfo: Repeatable with the Orchid Mantis Kebab after defeating the Hedge Broodmother first.

combatStats.attackDamageSummary:
Slash: 150
Charged strike: 175
Combo slam: 120
Ground shred: 90
Vertical shockwave: 120
Charged strike unearth: 80
Pounce: 100
Escape leap: 90
Scream: 10
